channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Promoting Economic Ties Between Oman and East Africa Through Kiswahili
item.description: Tanzania’s Ambassador to Oman, Maulidah Hassan, emphasized the role of Kiswahili in fostering economic cooperation between Oman and East African nations. She highlighted that the language, with its hi...

item.title: US strikes cause visible damage at Iran's Bushehr nuclear facility
item.description: Satellite imagery analysis reveals new damage to Iran's Bushehr nuclear power plant following US military strikes in July 2026. Al Jazeera Network compiled satellite images, field footage, and CENTCOM...

item.title: Ironbark Asset Management secures $225M investment from Merchant Investment Management
item.description: New York-based Merchant Investment Management has committed $225 million to Ironbark Asset Management, a firm overseeing over $90 billion across its funds management, trusteeship, and advisory divisio...

item.title: Senior Magistrate Martin Mutegi Relieved of Duties Over Bribe Allegations
item.description: Senior Principal Magistrate Martin Kinyua Mutegi of Kangema Law Court has been relieved of his duties following his arrest on allegations of soliciting and receiving a KSh 170,000 bribe from a litigan...

item.title: Ebola Outbreak in DRC and Uganda Claims Over 1,000 Lives, WHO Reports
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has confirmed nearly 2,500 cases of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) and Uganda, with over 999 deaths reported in DRC and two in Uganda. The outbreak...

item.title: S&P Global Ratings Upgrades Pakistan's Sovereign Credit Rating to 'B' with Stable Outlook
item.description: S&P Global Ratings has upgraded Pakistan's long-term sovereign credit rating from 'B-' to 'B', citing improvements in the country's external position and gradual macroeconomic stabilization. The agenc...

item.title: The Rift Between Those Who Left and Stayed in Ukraine: A Divided Reality
item.description: The article explores the emotional and psychological divide between Ukrainians who fled the war and those who remained, highlighting the silent rift that has grown over time. The author, a Ukrainian a...

item.title: Trump Faces Declining MAGA Support Amid Iran War Escalation
item.description: A new report reveals a dramatic decline in support for Donald Trump among his MAGA base, driven by the prolonged and costly Iran conflict. Polling data from Politico shows that 37% of MAGA voters now ...

item.title: Springboks coach Rassie Erasmus names 43-player squad for Argentina and All Blacks series, includes injured returns
item.description: South African rugby team coach Rassie Erasmus has announced a strategic approach by naming a 43-player squad to prepare for the Test against Argentina and the Rugby’s Greatest Rivalry series against N...

item.title: Bombay High Court Approves Tree Cutting for Transmission Lines in Maharashtra with Compensatory Measures
item.description: The Bombay High Court has approved the cutting of 21,022 trees and diversion of 94.68 hectares of forest land for constructing 132-KV DC transmission lines in Maharashtra’s Palghar district. The court...

item.title: APC leader criticizes Obi-Kwankwaso's presidential bid as weak and inexperienced
item.description: Former APC National Chairman, Abdullahi Ganduje, has publicly criticized the Peter Obi-Rabiu Kwankwaso presidential ticket, stating it lacks the political strength and governance experience needed to ...

item.title: Philippines accuses China of escalating tensions in West Philippine Sea
item.description: Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) spokesperson Rear Admiral Jay Tarriela strongly refuted Chinese Ambassador Jing Quan's claim that Philippine activities are causing tensions in the West Philippine Sea. Ta...

item.title: Rising Chicken Thigh Prices Drive Grocery Inflation in Canada
item.description: Prices for fresh or frozen chicken thighs in Canada surged 8.2% from May to June 2026, outpacing inflation for other staples like beef and coffee, according to Statistics Canada's consumer price index...

item.title: Historians criticise use of ancient artifacts in new film adaptation of The Odyssey
item.description: The original title appears to be a sensationalised clickbait headline focusing on the controversial use of ancient gold earrings to promote a reboot of Homer's 'Odyssey'. While the article seems to bl...

item.title: UK Ministry of Defence Admits to Past Human Radiation Experiments on Test Subjects
item.description: The UK Ministry of Defence has formally acknowledged conducting extensive human radiation experiments on military personnel and civilians during the Cold War era. These revelations, disclosed in a rec...

item.title: DCI Unveils Breakthrough in Investigation of TUK Lecturer's Murder
item.description: Detectives from Kenya's Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested Richard Gikuhe Mbugua, a key suspect in the murder of Edgar Mokua, a lecturer at Technical University of Kenya (TUK)....

item.title: Elvis Chipezeze commits to helping Magesi return to Premiership after relegation
item.description: Magesi goalkeeper Elvis Chipezeze has expressed his commitment to staying with the club as they navigate life in the Motsepe Foundation Championship (MFC) following their relegation from the Betway Pr...

item.title: Public Engagement Crucial for Impeachment Trial Success
item.description: The article discusses Franklin Drilon's emphasis on the importance of public engagement and civil society's role in ensuring accountability during the Sara Duterte impeachment trial. Drilon, a veteran...

item.title: Security forces kill 10 militants and arrest two female suicide bombers during intelligence-based operations in Balochistan
item.description: Pakistan's security forces carried out two intelligence-based operations (IBOs) in the Mastung and Surab districts of Balochistan over the past 48 hours, during which 10 militants were killed and two ...

item.title: Drones Target US Consulate in Iraq Amid Escalating Tensions
item.description: A drone attack near the U.S. consulate in Erbil, Iraq, has raised concerns about regional security and diplomatic safety. Reports indicate that drones struck the area surrounding the diplomatic facili...
